purposes.description: Create .xlsx files with headers, data rows, Excel formulas (SUM, AVERAGE), conditional formatting (red fill), currency formatting, and column widths using openpyxl.

primitives.evidence: Requires openpyxl (third-party library) for Workbook creation, PatternFill, Font styling, cell formatting, and formula insertion.
Must write a Python script to a .py file before execution (Critical Rule #2).
Workflow requires writing script to file then running with python3, verifying output with ls.
5 critical rules: exact filename, write to .py file (no python3 -c), use openpyxl, use Excel formulas not Python computation, apply PatternFill directly.
3-step workflow: write script, run it, verify output file exists.

purposes.description: Read existing .xlsx workbooks, analyze data, and modify them using openpyxl.

primitives.evidence: Requires openpyxl load_workbook for reading existing files, iterating sheets and cells.
Must write and execute Python script to read/modify Excel files.
Must save to exact requested filename.

purposes.description: Recalculate all formulas in an Excel file using LibreOffice and report formula errors.

primitives.evidence: recalc.py uses openpyxl, subprocess, platform, pathlib; manages LibreOffice macros, handles cross-platform differences.
Runs recalc.py with the Excel file as argument, parses JSON output.
